svg.text: KV STORE — WRITE PATH + CRASH RECOVERY
LANE A — WRITE PATH (steady state): log FIRST, then mutate under the lock
durability boundary — the log is the truth
READ PATH — get(key): read lock, lazy TTL check
isExpired ⇒ purge (upgrade to write lock) → MISS
lazy expiry is checked on the read;
keys that are never read again.
the same durable log feeds recovery →
LANE B — CRASH RECOVERY (startup): rebuild state from a snapshot base + WAL tail

g.text: so you only replay the WAL tail
Crash AFTER append, before the map
no harm. There is no window where
memory is ahead of the durable log.
WAL is an in-memory List and replay()
exists but isn't wired to startup yet;
path. On real disk you'd read the log
file + fsync. The SHAPE is the point.

svg.text: WRITE: ① put → ② append to WAL FIRST → take write lock → ③ store.put + recordInsert → ④ evictIfNeeded → ⑤ evict LRU + log it · RECOVERY: ⑥ startup → snapshot? load base → ⑦ replay WAL tail in order → ⑧ ready
KV store — WAL-first durability, LRU eviction under one write lock, lazy + swept TTL, snapshot + replay crash recovery — LLD
